---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---
```{r}
# Gibson-Schwartz (1997) Two-Factor Commodity Model
# Kalman Filter with Maximum Likelihood Estimation
# 
# This implementation follows Schwartz (1997) for analytical formulas of A(tau) and B(tau).
# Numerically robust version using pseudo-inverse and parameter bounds.

# Load required packages
if (!require(readxl, quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("readxl")
if (!require(ggplot2, quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("ggplot2")
if (!require(gridExtra, quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("gridExtra")
if (!require(Matrix, quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("Matrix")
if (!require(R6, quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("R6")
if (!require(MASS, quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("MASS")
if (!require(reshape2, quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("reshape2")

library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(Matrix)
library(R6)
library(MASS)
library(reshape2)

# ============================================================================
# GibsonSchwartz Class (implemented as R6 class)
# ============================================================================

GibsonSchwartz <- R6::R6Class(
  "GibsonSchwartz",
  
  public = list(
    # Public members
    spot_prices = NULL,
    futures_prices = NULL,
    maturities = NULL,
    risk_free_rates = NULL,
    dt = 1/252,
    n_obs = NULL,
    n_contracts = NULL,
    log_spot = NULL,
    log_futures = NULL,
    params = NULL,
    filtered_states = NULL,
    
    # Constructor
    initialize = function(spot_prices, futures_prices, maturities, risk_free_rates, dt = 1/252) {
      # Initialize the Gibson-Schwartz model
      self$spot_prices <- as.numeric(spot_prices)
      self$futures_prices <- as.matrix(futures_prices)
      self$maturities <- as.numeric(maturities)
      self$risk_free_rates <- as.numeric(risk_free_rates)
      self$dt <- dt
      self$n_obs <- length(spot_prices)
      self$n_contracts <- if(is.null(ncol(futures_prices))) 1 else ncol(futures_prices)
      
      # Check dimensions
      if (length(self$risk_free_rates) != self$n_obs) {
        stop(paste("risk_free_rates length (", length(self$risk_free_rates), 
                   ") must match observations (", self$n_obs, ")", sep = ""))
      }
      
      # Convert to log-spot and log-futures
      self$log_spot <- log(self$spot_prices)
      if (is.null(ncol(futures_prices))) {
        self$log_futures <- matrix(log(futures_prices), ncol = 1)
      } else {
        self$log_futures <- log(futures_prices)
      }
    },
    
    # B_tau method
    B_tau = function(tau, kappa) {
      result <- ifelse(kappa < 1e-6, tau, (1.0 - exp(-kappa * tau)) / kappa)
      return(result)
    },
    
    # A_tau method
    A_tau = function(tau, kappa, sigma_1, sigma_2, rho, r, delta_bar_q) {
      k <- kappa
      ss <- sigma_1
      sd <- sigma_2
      
      e1 <- exp(-k * tau)
      e2 <- exp(-2.0 * k * tau)
      
      b <- self$B_tau(tau, k)
      
      # Variance correction term
      term_delta <- sd^2 * (
        (tau / (k^2))
        - (2.0 * (1.0 - e1) / (k^3))
        + ((1.0 - e2) / (2.0 * k^3))
      )
      
      term_cross <- -2.0 * rho * ss * sd * (
        (tau / k) - ((1.0 - e1) / (k^2))
      )
      
      v <- term_delta + term_cross
      
      return(r * tau - delta_bar_q * (tau - b) + 0.5 * v)
    },
    
    # Kalman filter method
    kalman_filter = function(params) {
      # Unpack and clip parameters
      kappa <- pmax(pmin(params[1], 5.0), 0.01)
      sigma_1 <- pmax(pmin(params[2], 2.0), 0.01)
      sigma_2 <- pmax(pmin(params[3], 2.0), 0.01)
      rho <- pmax(pmin(params[4], 0.99), -0.99)
      lambda_param <- pmax(pmin(params[5], 0.5), -0.5)
      mu_s <- pmax(pmin(params[6], 1.0), 0.0)
      delta_bar_q <- pmax(pmin(params[7], 1.0), 0.0)
      
      tryCatch({
        # Build transition matrices (G and c for discrete-time)
        exp_k <- exp(-kappa * self$dt)
        G <- matrix(c(1.0, 0.0, -self$dt, exp_k), nrow = 2, ncol = 2, byrow = FALSE)
        
        # Process noise covariance Q (independent of time-varying r)
        var_x <- sigma_1^2 * self$dt
        var_delta <- sigma_2^2 * (1.0 - exp(-2.0 * kappa * self$dt)) / (2.0 * kappa + 1e-8)
        cov_x_delta <- rho * sigma_1 * sigma_2 * (1.0 - exp_k) / (kappa + 1e-8)
        
        Q <- matrix(c(var_x, cov_x_delta, cov_x_delta, var_delta), nrow = 2, ncol = 2, byrow = FALSE)
        
        # Ensure Q is positive definite
        Q <- (Q + t(Q)) / 2
        eigs_Q <- eigen(Q)$values
        if (any(eigs_Q < 1e-8)) {
          Q <- Q + diag(2) * (1e-7 - min(eigs_Q))
        }
        
        # Build measurement matrices
        B_vals <- sapply(self$maturities, function(tau) self$B_tau(tau, kappa))
        Z <- matrix(0, nrow = self$n_contracts, ncol = 2)
        Z[, 1] <- 1.0
        Z[, 2] <- -B_vals
        
        # Measurement noise covariance
        H <- diag(self$n_contracts) * 1e-3
        
        # Initialize filter
        x <- c(self$log_spot[1], mu_s)
        P <- diag(2) * 100.0
        
        log_likelihood <- 0.0
        n_valid <- 0
        
        # Kalman filter loop
        for (t in 1:self$n_obs) {
          # Get time-varying risk-free rate
          r_t <- self$risk_free_rates[t]
          
          # Update drift term in state equation with r_t
          c <- c(
            (r_t - lambda_param - 0.5 * sigma_1^2) * self$dt,
            mu_s * (1.0 - exp_k)
          )
          
          # Recompute d with current r_t (risk-neutral pricing)
          d <- sapply(self$maturities, function(tau) 
            self$A_tau(tau, kappa, sigma_1, sigma_2, rho, r_t, delta_bar_q)
          )
          
          # Prediction
          x_pred <- c + G %*% x
          P_pred <- G %*% P %*% t(G) + Q
          
          # Ensure P_pred is symmetric and positive definite
          P_pred <- (P_pred + t(P_pred)) / 2
          eigs_p <- eigen(P_pred)$values
          if (any(eigs_p < 1e-10)) {
            P_pred <- P_pred + diag(2) * (1e-9 - min(eigs_p))
          }
          
          # Measurement update
          y_pred <- d + Z %*% x_pred
          innovation <- self$log_futures[t, ] - y_pred
          
          # Innovation covariance
          S <- Z %*% P_pred %*% t(Z) + H
          S <- (S + t(S)) / 2
          
          # Use pseudo-inverse for numerical stability
          tryCatch({
            S_inv <- MASS::ginv(S)
            det_S <- det(S)
            
            if (det_S > 1e-30 && is.finite(det_S)) {
              # Kalman gain
              K <- P_pred %*% t(Z) %*% S_inv
              
              # Update state and covariance
              x <- x_pred + K %*% innovation
              P <- (diag(2) - K %*% Z) %*% P_pred
              
              # Log-likelihood contribution
              mahal <- as.numeric(t(innovation) %*% S_inv %*% innovation)
              if (is.finite(mahal) && mahal < 1000) {
                ll_t <- -0.5 * (self$n_contracts * log(2 * pi) + log(det_S) + mahal)
                if (is.finite(ll_t)) {
                  log_likelihood <- log_likelihood + ll_t
                  n_valid <- n_valid + 1
                }
              }
            }
          }, error = function(e) {
            # Silent catch
          })
        }
        
        if (n_valid < 50) {  # Need sufficient valid observations
          return(1e10)
        }
        
        # Normalize by number of valid observations
        avg_ll <- log_likelihood / n_valid
        
        # Return negative log-likelihood for minimization
        return(-avg_ll)
        
      }, error = function(e) {
        return(1e10)
      })
    },
    
    # Fit method
    fit = function(verbose = TRUE, method = 'L-BFGS-B') {
      # Initial parameter guess
      # Parameters: [kappa, sigma_1, sigma_2, rho, lambda, mu_s, delta_bar_q]
      initial_params <- c(
        0.5,     # kappa
        0.25,    # sigma_1
        0.25,    # sigma_2
        0.3,     # rho
        0.0,     # lambda
        0.05,    # mu_s
        0.05     # delta_bar_q
      )
      
      # Parameter bounds
      lower_bounds <- c(0.01, 0.01, 0.01, -0.99, -0.5, 0.0, 0.0)
      upper_bounds <- c(5.0, 2.0, 2.0, 0.99, 0.5, 1.0, 1.0)
      
      if (verbose) {
        cat("\nStarting MLE optimization...\n")
      }
      
      # Optimize
      result <- optim(
        initial_params,
        fn = function(p) self$kalman_filter(p),
        method = method,
        lower = lower_bounds,
        upper = upper_bounds,
        control = list(maxit = 200)
      )
      
      if (verbose) {
        cat("Optimization completed.\n")
        cat(sprintf("Final log-likelihood: %.6f\n", -result$value))
      }
      
      self$params <- result$par
      return(result)
    },
    
    # Print parameters method
    print_parameters = function() {
      if (is.null(self$params)) {
        cat("Model not fitted yet.\n")
        return()
      }
      
      param_names <- c('kappa', 'sigma_1', 'sigma_2', 'rho', 'lambda', 'mu_s', 'delta_bar_q')
      
      cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n")
      cat("GIBSON-SCHWARTZ MODEL - FITTED PARAMETERS (BRENT CRUDE OIL)\n")
      cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n")
      
      for (i in seq_along(param_names)) {
        cat(sprintf("%-20s: %12.8f\n", param_names[i], self$params[i]))
      }
      
      cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n")
      cat("\nParameter Interpretation:\n")
      cat(sprintf("  κ (mean reversion): %.4f - Controls speed of convenience yield reversion\n", self$params[1]))
      cat(sprintf("  σ₁ (spot vol):      %.4f - Volatility of log-spot price\n", self$params[2]))
      cat(sprintf("  σ₂ (delta vol):     %.4f - Volatility of convenience yield\n", self$params[3]))
      cat(sprintf("  ρ (correlation):    %.4f - Correlation between spot and delta shocks\n", self$params[4]))
      cat(sprintf("  λ (market price):   %.4f - Market price of risk\n", self$params[5]))
      cat(sprintf("  μₛ (long-run δ):    %.4f - Long-run convenience yield level\n", self$params[6]))
    },
    
    # Extract states method
    extract_states = function() {
      if (is.null(self$params)) {
        stop("Model not fitted. Call fit() first.")
      }
      
      kappa <- self$params[1]
      sigma_1 <- self$params[2]
      sigma_2 <- self$params[3]
      rho <- self$params[4]
      lambda_param <- self$params[5]
      mu_s <- self$params[6]
      delta_bar_q <- self$params[7]
      
      # Build matrices
      exp_k <- exp(-kappa * self$dt)
      G <- matrix(c(1.0, 0.0, -self$dt, exp_k), nrow = 2, ncol = 2, byrow = FALSE)
      
      var_x <- sigma_1^2 * self$dt
      var_delta <- sigma_2^2 * (1.0 - exp(-2.0 * kappa * self$dt)) / (2.0 * kappa + 1e-8)
      cov_x_delta <- rho * sigma_1 * sigma_2 * (1.0 - exp_k) / (kappa + 1e-8)
      Q <- matrix(c(var_x, cov_x_delta, cov_x_delta, var_delta), nrow = 2, ncol = 2, byrow = FALSE)
      Q <- (Q + t(Q)) / 2
      
      B_vals <- sapply(self$maturities, function(tau) self$B_tau(tau, kappa))
      Z <- matrix(0, nrow = self$n_contracts, ncol = 2)
      Z[, 1] <- 1.0
      Z[, 2] <- -B_vals
      
      H <- diag(self$n_contracts) * 1e-5
      
      # Initialize
      x <- c(self$log_spot[1], mu_s)
      P <- diag(2) * 100.0
      
      states <- matrix(0, nrow = self$n_obs, ncol = 2)
      
      # Filter loop
      for (t in 1:self$n_obs) {
        r_t <- self$risk_free_rates[t]
        
        c <- c(
          (r_t - lambda_param - 0.5 * sigma_1^2) * self$dt,
          mu_s * (1.0 - exp_k)
        )
        
        d <- sapply(self$maturities, function(tau) 
          self$A_tau(tau, kappa, sigma_1, sigma_2, rho, r_t, delta_bar_q)
        )
        
        x_pred <- c + G %*% x
        P_pred <- G %*% P %*% t(G) + Q
        P_pred <- (P_pred + t(P_pred)) / 2
        
        y_pred <- d + Z %*% x_pred
        innovation <- self$log_futures[t, ] - y_pred
        S <- Z %*% P_pred %*% t(Z) + H
        S <- (S + t(S)) / 2
        
        tryCatch({
          S_inv <- MASS::ginv(S)
          K <- P_pred %*% t(Z) %*% S_inv
          x <- x_pred + K %*% innovation
          P <- (diag(2) - K %*% Z) %*% P_pred
        }, error = function(e) {
          # Silent catch
        })
        
        states[t, ] <- x
      }
      
      self$filtered_states <- states
      spot_estimated <- exp(states[, 1])
      convenience_yield <- states[, 2]
      
      return(list(spot = spot_estimated, delta = convenience_yield))
    },
    
    # Plot results method
    plot_results = function() {
      if (is.null(self$filtered_states)) {
        result <- self$extract_states()
      }
      
      spot_est <- exp(self$filtered_states[, 1])
      dy <- self$filtered_states[, 2]
      time_idx <- 1:length(self$spot_prices)
      
      # Create data frame for plotting
      df_plot <- data.frame(
        time = c(time_idx, time_idx),
        price = c(self$spot_prices, spot_est),
        type = c(rep("Actual", length(time_idx)), rep("Estimated", length(time_idx)))
      )
      
      df_dy <- data.frame(
        time = time_idx,
        delta = dy
      )
      
      # Plot 1: Spot Price
      p1 <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = time, y = price, color = type, linetype = type)) +
        geom_line(size = 0.8) +
        scale_color_manual(values = c("Actual" = "blue", "Estimated" = "red")) +
        scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Actual" = "solid", "Estimated" = "dashed")) +
        labs(
          x = "Time (days)",
          y = "Price ($/barrel)",
          title = "Brent Crude Oil: Actual vs Filtered Spot Price"
        ) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
          legend.position = "top"
        )
      
      rmse_spot <- sqrt(mean((self$spot_prices - spot_est)^2))
      p1 <- p1 + annotate("text", x = Inf, y = -Inf, 
                          label = paste0("RMSE: $", round(rmse_spot, 4), "/barrel"),
                          hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5, size = 3, color = "black")
      
      # Plot 2: Convenience Yield
      p2 <- ggplot(df_dy, aes(x = time, y = delta)) +
        geom_line(color = "green", size = 0.8) +
        geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = 0, ymax = pmax(delta, 0)), fill = "green", alpha = 0.3) +
        geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = pmin(delta, 0), ymax = 0), fill = "red", alpha = 0.3) +
        geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", size = 0.5) +
        labs(
          x = "Time (days)",
          y = "Convenience Yield",
          title = "Brent Crude Oil: Estimated Instantaneous Convenience Yield"
        ) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
        )
      
      # Combine plots
      combined_plot <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(p1, p2, nrow = 2, heights = c(1, 1))
      
      ggsave("gs_model_results.png", combined_plot, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 150)
      cat("Plot saved: gs_model_results.png\n")
    },
    
    # Plot futures method
    plot_futures = function() {
      if (is.null(self$params)) {
        stop("Model not fitted.")
      }
      
      kappa <- self$params[1]
      sigma_1 <- self$params[2]
      sigma_2 <- self$params[3]
      rho <- self$params[4]
      lambda_param <- self$params[5]
      mu_s <- self$params[6]
      delta_bar_q <- self$params[7]
      
      B_vals <- sapply(self$maturities, function(tau) self$B_tau(tau, kappa))
      Z <- matrix(0, nrow = self$n_contracts, ncol = 2)
      Z[, 1] <- 1.0
      Z[, 2] <- -B_vals
      
      if (is.null(self$filtered_states)) {
        self$extract_states()
      }
      
      # Compute fitted log-futures for each time step with time-varying r
      exp_k <- exp(-kappa * self$dt)
      fitted_list <- list()
      
      for (t in 1:self$n_obs) {
        r_t <- self$risk_free_rates[t]
        d <- sapply(self$maturities, function(tau) 
          self$A_tau(tau, kappa, sigma_1, sigma_2, rho, r_t, delta_bar_q)
        )
        log_fitted_t <- d + Z %*% self$filtered_states[t, ]
        fitted_list[[t]] <- exp(log_fitted_t)
      }
      
      fitted <- do.call(rbind, fitted_list)
      
      # Create plots for each contract
      n_cont <- self$n_contracts
      n_rows <- ceiling(n_cont / 2)
      
      plots_list <- list()
      
      for (i in 1:n_cont) {
        actual <- self$futures_prices[, i]
        df_futures <- data.frame(
          time = 1:self$n_obs,
          actual = actual,
          fitted = fitted[, i]
        )
        
        df_futures_long <- reshape2::melt(df_futures, id.vars = "time")
        
        p <- ggplot(df_futures_long, aes(x = time, y = value, color = variable, linetype = variable)) +
          geom_line(size = 0.8) +
          scale_color_manual(values = c("actual" = "blue", "fitted" = "red")) +
          scale_linetype_manual(values = c("actual" = "solid", "fitted" = "dashed")) +
          labs(
            x = "Time (days)",
            y = "Futures Price ($/barrel)",
            title = sprintf("Brent Contract %d (τ = %.3f yrs)", i, self$maturities[i])
          ) +
          theme_minimal() +
          theme(
            plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
            legend.position = "top"
          )
        
        rmse <- sqrt(mean((actual - fitted[, i])^2))
        p <- p + annotate("text", x = Inf, y = -Inf, 
                         label = paste0("RMSE: ", round(rmse, 4)),
                         hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5, size = 3, color = "black")
        
        plots_list[[i]] <- p
      }
      
      # Combine plots
      combined_plot <- do.call(gridExtra::grid.arrange, c(plots_list, list(nrow = n_rows)))
      
      ggsave("gs_futures_comparison.png", combined_plot, width = 15, height = 4 * n_rows, dpi = 150)
      cat("Plot saved: gs_futures_comparison.png\n")
    }
  )
)

# ============================================================================
# Main Function
# ============================================================================

main <- function() {
  cat("\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n")
  cat("GIBSON-SCHWARTZ TWO-FACTOR COMMODITY PRICING MODEL\n")
  cat("BRENT CRUDE OIL ANALYSIS\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n")
  
  # Load data
  cat("\nLoading Brent time series data...\n")
  
  # Check if file exists
  excel_file <- 'TimesSeries_BRENT.xlsx'
  if (!file.exists(excel_file)) {
    cat("ERROR: File '", excel_file, "' not found in working directory!\n", sep = "")
    cat("Current working directory: ", getwd(), "\n", sep = "")
    cat("Please ensure TimesSeries_BRENT.xlsx is in the working directory.\n")
    return()
  }
  
  df <- NULL
  tryCatch({
    # Try to read from Excel file with 'Feuil1' sheet (French for Sheet1)
    df <- read_excel(excel_file, sheet = 'Feuil1')
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Note: Could not read 'Feuil1' sheet, trying default sheet...\n")
  })
  
  if (is.null(df)) {
    tryCatch({
      # Fallback to first sheet if 'Feuil1' doesn't exist
      df <- read_excel(excel_file, sheet = 1)
    }, error = function(e2) {
      cat("ERROR: Could not read Excel file!\n")
      cat("Error message: ", e2$message, "\n", sep = "")
      return()
    })
  }
  
  if (is.null(df)) {
    cat("ERROR: Failed to load data.\n")
    return()
  }
  
  cat("✓ Data loaded successfully\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Dimensions: %d rows × %d columns\n", nrow(df), ncol(df)))
  cat(sprintf("  Available columns: %s\n", paste(names(df), collapse = ", ")))
  
  # Extract spot prices from F1 (first futures contract as proxy for spot)
  if (!("F1" %in% names(df))) {
    cat("ERROR: Column 'F1' not found in data\n")
    cat("Available columns are: ", paste(names(df), collapse = ", "), "\n", sep = "")
    return()
  }
  
  # Get clean indices (remove NaN from spot AND risk-free rates simultaneously)
  spot_raw <- as.numeric(df$F1)
  valid_idx <- !is.na(spot_raw)
  spot <- spot_raw[valid_idx]
  
  cat(sprintf("✓ Extracted %d spot prices from column F1\n", length(spot)))
  cat(sprintf("  Spot price range: $%.2f - $%.2f\n", min(spot, na.rm = TRUE), max(spot, na.rm = TRUE)))
  
  # Get risk-free rates and apply same valid indices
  if ("T-Bill_1M" %in% names(df)) {
    risk_free_rates_raw <- as.numeric(df[["T-Bill_1M"]])
    
    if (length(risk_free_rates_raw) >= length(spot_raw)) {
      risk_free_rates <- risk_free_rates_raw[valid_idx]
      
      # Convert to decimal if in percentage (assume > 0.5 means percentage)
      if (max(risk_free_rates, na.rm = TRUE) > 0.5) {
        risk_free_rates <- risk_free_rates / 100.0
        cat("✓ Risk-free rates converted from percentage to decimal\n")
      }
      
      # Handle any NaN values in risk-free rates by forward-filling
      for (i in seq_along(risk_free_rates)) {
        if (is.na(risk_free_rates[i])) {
          if (i > 1) {
            risk_free_rates[i] <- risk_free_rates[i - 1]
          } else {
            risk_free_rates[i] <- 0.02
          }
        }
      }
    } else {
      cat("⚠ Warning: Insufficient T-Bill_1M data. Using default 2% rate.\n")
      risk_free_rates <- rep(0.02, length(spot))
    }
  } else {
    cat("⚠ Warning: Column 'T-Bill_1M' not found. Using default 2% rate.\n")
    risk_free_rates <- rep(0.02, length(spot))
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("✓ Risk-free rate range: %.6f - %.6f\n", min(risk_free_rates), max(risk_free_rates)))
  
  # Extract futures prices and maturities
  futures_list <- list()
  maturities <- c()
  
  # Futures contracts: F1, F2, F3, F4, F5 with corresponding maturities
  futures_cols <- c('F1', 'F2', 'F3', 'F4', 'F5')
  
  # Maturity mapping for Brent crude futures (typical tenor structure)
  maturity_dict <- list(
    F1 = 1/12,      # 1 month
    F2 = 5/12,      # 5 months
    F3 = 9/12,      # 9 months
    F4 = 13/12,     # 13 months
    F5 = 17/12      # 17 months
  )
  
  cat("\nLoading futures contracts:\n")
  for (col in futures_cols) {
    if (col %in% names(df)) {
      tryCatch({
        prices_raw <- as.numeric(df[[col]])
        prices <- prices_raw[valid_idx]
        
        if (length(prices) == length(spot)) {
          futures_list[[col]] <- prices
          maturities <- c(maturities, maturity_dict[[col]])
          cat(sprintf("  ✓ %s: tau = %.3f years (%.0f months)\n", 
                     col, maturity_dict[[col]], maturity_dict[[col]] * 12))
        }
      }, error = function(e) {
        cat(sprintf("  ✗ %s: Could not load - %s\n", col, e$message))
      })
    }
  }
  
  if (length(futures_list) == 0) {
    cat("ERROR: No futures data could be loaded\n")
    return()
  }
  
  futures <- do.call(cbind, futures_list)
  maturities <- as.numeric(maturities)
  
  cat(sprintf("\n  ✓ Observations: %d\n", length(spot)))
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ Spot price range: $%.2f - $%.2f\n", min(spot), max(spot)))
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ Futures contracts loaded: %d\n", length(maturities)))
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ Maturities: %s\n", paste(round(maturities, 3), collapse = ", ")))
  
  # Create and fit model
  cat("\nInitializing Gibson-Schwartz model...\n")
  tryCatch({
    model <- GibsonSchwartz$new(spot, futures, maturities, risk_free_rates)
    cat("✓ Model initialized successfully\n")
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("ERROR: Could not initialize model - ", e$message, "\n", sep = "")
    return()
  })
  
  cat("\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n")
  cat("MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATION\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n")
  
  tryCatch({
    model$fit(verbose = TRUE)
    model$print_parameters()
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("ERROR: Optimization failed - ", e$message, "\n", sep = "")
    return()
  })
  
  # Extract states and plot
  cat("\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n")
  cat("GENERATING RESULTS\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n")
  
  tryCatch({
    states_result <- model$extract_states()
    spot_est <- states_result$spot
    dy <- states_result$delta
    
    cat("✓ States extracted successfully\n")
    cat("Generating plots...\n")
    model$plot_results()
    model$plot_futures()
    cat("✓ Plots generated successfully\n")
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("ERROR: Could not extract states or generate plots - ", e$message, "\n", sep = "")
    return()
  })
  
  # Summary
  cat("\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n")
  cat("SUMMARY STATISTICS\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n")
  cat(sprintf("Spot price RMSE: $%.4f/barrel\n", sqrt(mean((spot - spot_est)^2))))
  cat(sprintf("Convenience yield: %.6f to %.6f\n", min(dy), max(dy)))
  cat(sprintf("Mean convenience yield: %.6f\n", mean(dy)))
  cat(strrep("=", 65), "\n\n")
  cat("✓ ANALYSIS COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY\n\n")
}

# Run the main function - always execute
main()


```
